title: POST /api/panes and POST /api/agents: the create routes
action:
Add src/web/create.rs with two routes, registered in the table at src/web/mod.rs:51-61. POST /api/panes takes {workspace_id} and calls tab_create. POST /api/agents takes {workspace_id, preset} -- a preset LABEL, never argv (P6) -- looks the label up in AppState's preset list and calls agent_start with the argv that came from config. Copy send_reply's shape (src/web/screen.rs:56-71): _auth: AuthSession first, then State, then Json.

THE UNRESOLVED-PATH BRANCH IS ASYMMETRIC, and this is the correctness heart of the cell. Both routes seed the create with the destination's anchor path when it resolves. When it does NOT resolve: the SHELL route omits cwd, because herdr then resolves the workspace's own anchor exactly as the desktop does (upstreams/herdr/src/app/api/tabs.rs:65-67). The AGENT route must NOT omit it -- agent.start's omit-cwd fallback is the herdr process's own directory (upstreams/herdr/src/app/agents.rs:118-122), an arbitrary folder, which is precisely the silent wrong-repo start parent D5 forbids. An agent create into a destination whose path did not resolve is refused with 409 and a message naming the reason. The plan text claiming both verbs fall back alike is wrong and was corrected here after validation; do not restore the symmetry.

Error mapping per the CONTEXT table: HerdrError::WorkspaceNotFound, and Remote with code agent_placement_not_found or agent_placement_conflict -> 409; unknown preset label -> 400; unresolved destination path on the agent route -> 409; everything else -> 502 {error: ...}. 404 stays reserved for the unauthenticated answer (P7). Success is 200 with the ids the port returned, plus the generated name for an agent. Name the new tests with the prefix createroute_.

truths: A request body cannot influence what is executed: argv is never a deserialized field, and an unknown preset label is refused before the port is called
The argv handed to the port is the one from config, byte-for-byte
Both routes, unauthenticated, return a bare 404
A destination whose path did not resolve still creates a SHELL, with cwd omitted
A destination whose path did not resolve REFUSES an agent with 409 rather than starting it in an arbitrary folder
Every pre-existing web test stays green
artifacts: src/web/create.rs with both handlers and their error mapping
Both routes registered in the existing table

At least 7 tests prefixed createroute_, all passing: shell create, agent create, unknown preset, stale destination, generic port failure, unauthenticated on both routes, and both sides of the unresolved-path branch
key_links: upstreams/herdr/src/app/agents.rs:118-122 -- the process-cwd fallback the agent branch must never reach
upstreams/herdr/src/app/api/tabs.rs:65-67 -- the anchor fallback the shell branch relies on
prohibitions: Do not accept argv, env, or any command-shaped field from the client in any form
Do not omit cwd on the agent route -- ever
Do not reimplement or surface the name-collision retry -- it lives inside the port
Do not return 404 for a missing workspace
Do not touch the frontend -- no FAB, no sheet, no web/src change (slice 5)

verify: cargo test --quiet && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --lib -- createroute_ 2>&1 | grep -qE 'test result: ok\. ([7-9]|[1-9][0-9]+) passed'

trace.outcome: Added src/web/create.rs with POST /api/panes (shell) and POST /api/agents (agent), registered in the web route table. Shell omits cwd on an unresolved anchor; agent refuses with 409. Preset resolved by label, argv from config only. 9 createroute_ tests.
